fields.summary: Move health check script related functionality to hadoop-common

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description:
HDFS might want to leverage health check functionality available in YARN in both namenode https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/HDFS-7400 and datanode https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/HDFS-7441.

We can move health check functionality including the protocol between hadoop daemons and health check script to hadoop-common. That will simplify the development and maintenance for both hadoop source code and health check script.

Thoughts?

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: NodeHealthScriptRunner.shouldRun() check is performing 3 times for starting NodeHealthScriptRunner

Test Failure unrelated. Passing in local.
Findbugs to be addressed by other JIRAs.
[~mingma], kindly look at the patch. Going by the patch for HDFS-7400, it seems LocalDirsHandlerService is not required hence that would still be part of nodemanager.
[~aw], kindly review

The only thing that concerns me is whether or not we should classify this service beyond private.  I think for now this is fine though and we can revisit that later.  Pending a re-run of the jenkins tests, I'm +1.

[~varun_saxena], sorry for the late response. Couple comments:

1. It will be useful if we can have some unit test for {{NodeHealthScriptRunner}} under hadoop-common-project. Maybe we can move {{TestNodeHealthService}} from hadoop-yarn.
2. {{NodeHealthStatus}} seems general. For HDFS NN's health check, this object could be handy if we want to show the health check on NN webUI. More interesting case is health check for HDFS DN; this structure can be reused for RPC between DN and NN. We can use another jira for that, move {{NodeHealthStatus}} and the protobuf defintion to hadoop-common.
3. HDFS so far hasn't used AbstractService. NodeHealthScriptRunner will be the first. That seems ok. Still, raise the point in case.

Or, you could go look at YARN-3375 .
The target version is on 2.x, why we commit to trunk only? This doesn't sounds like incompatibility change. Reopen it until we commit it to branch-2 together with YARN-3375.
We should make this in 2.8 after YARN-3375 get committed. Marked this to be blocker since it will cause unnecessary code divergence between trunk/branch-2.
Committed to branch-2 also, closing this ticket.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

Test Failure unrelated. Passing in local.
Findbugs to be addressed by other JIRAs.
[~mingma], kindly look at the patch. Going by the patch for HDFS-7400, it seems LocalDirsHandlerService is not required hence that would still be part of nodemanager.
[~aw], kindly review

The only thing that concerns me is whether or not we should classify this service beyond private.  I think for now this is fine though and we can revisit that later.  Pending a re-run of the jenkins tests, I'm +1.

[~varun_saxena], sorry for the late response. Couple comments:

1. It will be useful if we can have some unit test for {{NodeHealthScriptRunner}} under hadoop-common-project. Maybe we can move {{TestNodeHealthService}} from hadoop-yarn.
2. {{NodeHealthStatus}} seems general. For HDFS NN's health check, this object could be handy if we want to show the health check on NN webUI. More interesting case is health check for HDFS DN; this structure can be reused for RPC between DN and NN. We can use another jira for that, move {{NodeHealthStatus}} and the protobuf defintion to hadoop-common.
3. HDFS so far hasn't used AbstractService. NodeHealthScriptRunner will be the first. That seems ok. Still, raise the point in case.
